destinations.evidence: Both execution paths call the configured block verifier on the sealed block before post-execution finalization; verifier failure is converted into BlockExecutionError and aborts execution.

equivalence_argument: The v2.2 nodebuilder replaces the upstream BasicBlockExecutor surface with RitualBlockExecutor, but preserves the invariant behavior: after all transactions execute and before apply_post_execution_changes, the configured verifier is invoked against the sealed block and any verifier error aborts block execution.
notes: Mapped to the nodebuilder dependency because the executable Ritual executor wrapper owns the verifier invocation in the v2.2 architecture.
rationale: The primary diff adds block_verifier plumbing to the executor provider/executor and inserts verification before post-execution state finalization, making verifier failure part of block execution outcome. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: When a BasicBlockExecutor has a block_verifier configured, execute_one and execute_one_with_state_hook call verifier.verify_block on the sealed block after executing all transactions and before apply_post_execution_changes; verifier errors abort execution as BlockExecutionError.
suggested_falsification_tests: Configure a BasicBlockExecutor with a mock verifier that returns an error, execute a block, and assert apply_post_execution_changes is not reached and execution returns a BlockExecutionError containing the verifier failure.
summary: RitualBlockExecutor runs the configured block verifier before post-execution finalization.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Install a verifier double in RitualBlockExecutorProvider that returns an error and assert execute_one and execute_one_with_state_hook return BlockExecutionError before finalization.
title: Executor runs configured block verifier
